title: Split domus task and idea commands into multiple files
summary: task.ts and idea.ts are single large files with all subcommand logic inline. Refactor each into a directory (e.g. commands/task/ and commands/idea/) with one file per subcommand plus a shared helpers file.

title: Fix domus CLI access in Claude Code Bash tool
summary: The Bash tool runs with a stripped PATH that excludes ~/.bun/bin, so 'domus' is not found. Options: add ~/.bun/bin to the tool's PATH via shell init, use DOMUS=/Users/kion/.bun/bin/domus alias in CLAUDE.md, or document the full path workaround.
outcome_note: Added explicit PATH env block to ~/.claude/settings.json including ~/.bun/bin — domus and bun now resolve in all Bash tool sessions without symlinks

title: Fix task status updates to go through domus CLI
summary: Skills and Claude currently update task status by editing markdown files directly, bypassing domus task status and leaving tasks.jsonl out of sync. Fix by ensuring domus task status works correctly and updating skill instructions to shell out to the CLI.

title: domus task status should update both tasks.jsonl and task markdown frontmatter
summary: When domus task status runs, it updates tasks.jsonl but leaves the Status field in the task's markdown detail file stale. It should update both atomically so the md file stays in sync without Claude needing to touch it.

title: Add domus task/idea show command and --json flag on list
summary: Add --json flag to task/idea list for machine-readable bulk output, and a show subcommand for human-readable single-entry detail. Eliminates the need to read .md or .jsonl files directly.

title: Write unit tests for domus CLI
summary: Add a bun test suite covering the core logic in task.ts and idea.ts: JSONL read/write, ID generation, status transitions, flag parsing, frontmatter sync. Run against temp directories to avoid polluting real .domus/ state.

title: Add domus task/idea update command for editing metadata fields
summary: No CLI command exists for updating fields like title, summary, tags, or priority after creation. Direct edits to .jsonl bypass the CLI and break the contract that index files are never touched directly.

title: Fix worker isProcessRunning receives worktreePath instead of pid
summary: pollWorker passes worktreePath (a string) to isProcessRunning which expects a pid. parseInt on a path is NaN so process.kill(NaN, 0) throws, marking every running worker failed immediately. Fix: add pid to WorkerStatus, persist in dispatchWorker, pass correctly.
outcome_note: Worker dispatch feature not in use yet

title: Fix worktreeHasNewCommits hardcodes master as base branch
summary: git log master..HEAD silently fails on main-based repos, causing all completed workers to be classified as failed even when commits were made. Base branch should come from project git config or be passed as context.
outcome_note: Worker dispatch feature not in use yet

title: Rework domus init to handle current workspace structure and be idempotent
summary: Rework domus init for the project-local model: creates missing .domus/ subdirs and seed files, merges .claude/settings.json (permissions + PATH), reports what was created vs skipped, never destructive. Removes global config model.

title: Fix temp directory leak in tickets.test.ts on test failure
summary: parseTicket and updateTicketStatus test blocks use Date.now() for unique dirs with inline cleanup. If an assertion fails before cleanup, the directory leaks. Use beforeEach/afterEach or try/finally for guaranteed cleanup.

title: domus commands walk up to find .domus directory
summary: Non-init domus commands should traverse parent directories to find the nearest .domus/ dir, like git finds .git. Currently resolveWorkspace() reads from global config. Replace with upward traversal from cwd so domus works from any subdirectory of a project.

title: Migrate domus-specific ideas and tasks from everycent to domus
summary: Domus work has been flowing through everycent for dev experience reasons. Once domus is stable enough to self-host, move all domus-specific ideas, tasks, and memory content back to the domus project context.

title: Add --root flag to domus commands to target a specific project
summary: domus commands resolve to cwd/.domus/, making it impossible to target a different project without cd-ing first. Add a --root <path> flag (or env var) so commands like task add and idea add can write to a specified project root — needed while building domus from within the everycent project context.

title: Get domus ready for prod / new machine setup
summary: Three deliverables: (1) setup/skills/ with all required Claude Code skill SKILL.md files for copying to ~/.claude/skills/, (2) setup/agents-md-instructions/ with the content block to paste into agents.md/CLAUDE.md, (3) README.md covering concepts, vision, disclaimer (learning project), global install steps, and per-project setup steps. domus init already handles PATH and .claude/settings.json — document it, don't change it.

title: Add priority field to domus task/idea list output
summary: The human-readable list output shows icon, refinement, id, and title but omits priority. Adding priority to the list line makes it visible at a glance without needing --json, since it directly affects what to work on next.

title: Add domus CLI and store permissions to domus init
summary: domus commands (via domus or bun) and .domus/ file access should never trigger permission prompts in Claude Code. domus init should write the correct permissions into .claude/settings.json so any project can apply them by running domus init.

title: Add --depends-on flag to domus task update
summary: task update supports title, summary, tags, priority, refinement but not depends_on. Adding --depends-on allows dependencies to be set or changed after creation without direct file edits.

title: Add Edit and Write tool permissions to domus init
summary: Background agents need Edit and Write permissions pre-authorized in settings.json to avoid pulling focus back to the foreground. domus init should include these alongside the existing Bash/Read/Glob/Grep entries.
outcome_note: Already implemented — Edit and Write were already in REQUIRED_PERMISSIONS in src/commands/init.ts.

title: Fix resolveDomusPermission to work with bun linked binaries
summary: bun link sets process.argv[1] to the .ts source file even for installed binaries, so the .ts suffix check causes resolveDomusPermission to return null. Need a different detection strategy — e.g. resolve the binary from PATH, or use an env var set by the CLI wrapper.

title: Fix --help exiting with code 1 instead of 0
summary: All --help invocations (e.g. domus task add --help) print usage correctly but exit with code 1. Should exit 0 so callers (shell scripts, Claude tools) don't treat it as a failure.

title: Add --depends-on flag to task update command
summary: The task update CLI command is missing --depends-on support. Only task add has it. This means dependencies can't be set on existing tasks via CLI, forcing direct file edits.

title: Add --depends-on support to domus task update
summary: domus task update has no --depends-on flag, so dependency relationships can't be set after task creation. Should support: domus task update <id> --depends-on <id1,id2> and ideally domus task update <id> --add-depends-on <id> / --remove-depends-on <id> for incremental edits.
outcome_note: Duplicate of add-depends-on-flag-to-domus-task-update which is already autonomous

title: Add execution log command for autonomous task progress
summary: Add a CLI command for agents to append timestamped progress entries to a task's execution log. Log lives separately from the task body — separate file, domus owns writes, Claude reads to resume. Convention: in-progress or stalled tasks have a log at .domus/logs/<id>.md. Exact command shape, log format, and cold-agent orientation convention TBD in refinement.

title: Add stalled task status for human-in-the-loop escalation
summary: Add a 'stalled' status to the task lifecycle for tasks that are in-progress but blocked waiting for human input. Agent marks task stalled with context (what was tried, what's needed), human sees it, answers, agent resumes. Distinct from cancelled/deferred — it's a temporary pause expecting a response. Details of the escalation UX and status transitions TBD in refinement.

title: Add pipeline observability command
summary: Add a CLI command (domus task pipeline or similar) that shows the live execution view: what's running, what's stalled with context, what's queued by dependency order. The data foundation is execution logs + task status. Complements domus task list (which is the full inventory) by focusing on active state. Exact output format and filtering TBD in refinement.

title: Update domus CLAUDE.md with vision and pillar framing
summary: The vision articulated in the 2026-03-15 session belongs in domus CLAUDE.md so every session starts with it as context. Core: remove human from execution path without removing them from decision path. Four pillars: interactive session support, concept refinement engine, auto execution engine, observability platform. Context mobility as the substrate beneath all four. Also update domus-vision.md in memory to reflect this framing.

title: Move WORKER_NOTES.md into .domus/ directory
summary: WORKER_NOTES.md currently lives at the repo root but belongs in .domus/ alongside other domus-managed files. Update init to seed .domus/WORKER_NOTES.md, and update agents.md and any persona files that reference the old location.

title: Add domus task overview command
summary: A compact, watch-friendly task overview grouped into Supervised and Autonomous sections. Supervised tasks show priority (▲ · ▼) and refinement (~ raw, ◎ refined) icons. Autonomous tasks show priority only. Status shown via icon (○ open, ◑ in-progress) with ANSI colours — pick reasonable defaults, can be adjusted. Terminal wrapping handled natively (no truncation). Default: open/in-progress tasks only. Flags: --include-done, --blocked. Designed to run via watch loop: watch domus task overview.

title: Add domus task watch command
summary: Convenience wrapper around 'watch -c -t -n <interval> domus task overview'. Supports --interval flag (default 15s) passed through to watch -n and to the overview command for display. Overview shows a dimmed refresh indicator (e.g. ↻ 15s) as the first line when --interval is set.

title: Add --note, --parent, --idea flags to domus task update
summary: task update is missing three flags that task add supports: --note (outcome_note), --parent (parent_id), and --idea (idea_id). All three should be updatable after capture without direct file edits.

title: Add 'proposed' refinement status between raw and refined
summary: Add 'proposed' as a new TaskRefinement value: raw → proposed → refined → autonomous. Proposed means Claude did a refinement pass (acceptance criteria written, shape clear) but the human hasn't reviewed yet. Update type, validation, help text, overview icons (◐ for proposed), and tests.

title: Add DOMUS_ROOT to domus init config
summary: domus init should write DOMUS_ROOT (the main repo path) to a config file in .domus/ so any domus command — including those running inside a worktree — can resolve the main store path without it being passed explicitly. This is the foundation for all worker logging.

title: Add domus task log command
summary: Add a domus task log <id> <message> command that writes to both the task execution log (.domus/execution-logs/<id>.md) and appends a structured event to the audit log (.domus/audit.jsonl). Includes worker UUID (set by dispatch) and timestamp. Single call, both consumers satisfied.
outcome_note: Commands implemented in feat/rework-domus-init. DOMUS_ROOT resolution criterion now met by worker-autonomy-and-execution-model-alignment (projectRoot() updated to 3-tier resolution).

title: domus dispatch owns full worker lifecycle
summary: Extend domus dispatch to own the full worker lifecycle: claim task (atomic in-progress status update with file lock), record worktree branch on task, set DOMUS_ROOT, generate and pass worker UUID, emit started event via domus task log, emit done/stalled event on exit. No supported path to start a worker that bypasses dispatch.
outcome_note: Superseded by rework-domus-dispatch-to-align-with-execution-engine-architecture — new architecture is much simpler, domus does not own worktree/lifecycle management

title: Add dispatch skill for autonomous task execution
summary: A skill that intercepts attempts to execute an autonomous task and routes them through domus dispatch. Ensures worker UUID, DOMUS_ROOT, branch recording, and audit events are always set up correctly. Improves over time to capture more trigger patterns. Must also prompt the operator if they appear to be on main/master before dispatching.
outcome_note: Superseded — routing logic moves into foreman skill, which will be captured as a new task during dispatch rework refinement

title: Add protected branch rule to agents.md
summary: Add an explicit rule to agents.md: before dispatching workers, confirm you are not on main/master. If the human hasn't created a branch, prompt them to do so first. Workers branch off the current HEAD — if that's a protected branch, their work can't be merged back cleanly.

title: Define execution steps as part of auto task refinement
summary: When refining an autonomous task, the output should include not just acceptance criteria but also a defined step list — the checkpoints the worker will log via domus task log. Steps make execution logs useful for resumability and give the human visibility into progress granularity. Needs a convention for how steps are expressed in the task markdown and how workers reference them.

title: migrate dispatch from ticket format to task file format
fix skill frontmatter triggers to cover agent-invocation scenarios
add docs/cli-reference.md with domus CLI usage patterns
add blocked section to domus task overview
refactor domus init to install template files from source instead of embedded strings
add truncation indicator to domus task watch when output exceeds terminal height
add reviewing status for work completed in worktree awaiting human merge
summary: No current distinction between open, in-progress, and done-but-pending-review. Need a status that represents worker done and branch exists but human hasn't reviewed or merged yet. Affects overview visibility and task lifecycle clarity.
outcome_note: Superseded by ADR 005 state machine — replaced by ready-for-senior-review and ready-for-manual-review statuses, captured in add-execution-engine-status-values-to-task-cli task

title: Rework domus dispatch to align with execution engine architecture
summary: Current dispatch.ts is overcomplicated — project registry lookup, context assembly, manual worktree/PID management. Rework to be a thin trigger: validate task, call domus task start, hand off to Claude via foreman skill. Worktree management is Claude's job.
outcome_note: Implemented in feat/rework-domus-init: dispatch is now a thin trigger, calls task start for open tasks, resumes for in-progress, no lifecycle/worktree machinery. domus-dispatch-owns-full-worker-lifecycle already cancelled.

title: Add execution engine status values to task CLI
summary: ADR 005 defines two new task statuses — ready-for-senior-review and ready-for-manual-review — that the task CLI does not yet know about. Add them as valid status values with correct transition rules.
outcome_note: Implemented in feat/rework-domus-init: ready-for-senior-review transition added, icons, tests, agent-instructions.md updated.

title: Settle executor/reviewer persona terminology and update ADR 005
summary: Decide whether 'senior reviewer' or 'reviewer' is the right name for the reviewer persona. Once decided, update ADR 005 and any other references accordingly.

title: Create foreman skill
summary: Implement the foreman skill that routes autonomous worker invocations based on task status and execution log. Includes shared capabilities (Route, Advance) and human-facing capabilities (Send Back, Merge and Close) as defined in ADR 005.

title: Create worker persona
summary: Implement the worker persona — the autonomous executor that implements tasks, logs progress, and transitions task state. Includes the foreman skill and routes to executor and reviewer specialisations.

title: Create Taskmaster persona
summary: Implement the Taskmaster persona — clarifies vague tasks through exploration, then produces a precise execution plan the worker can run autonomously. Always clarifies before planning, never guesses.

title: Create Doctor persona
summary: Implement the Doctor persona — works interactively with the human to surface and resolve stale tasks, outdated memory, inconsistent decisions, and general .domus/ store health.

title: Create dispatch skill
summary: Implement the dispatch skill — natural language interface for handing a refined task off to a worker for autonomous execution, or resuming an interrupted task. Wraps domus dispatch with intent-based triggering.

title: Create update skill for tasks and ideas
summary: Implement a single skill that handles updates to existing tasks and ideas via natural language. The skill infers what kind of update is needed from the user's intent and applies it via the appropriate domus CLI commands.

title: Show dependency chains in blocked section of task overview
summary: Refactor the blocked section of domus task overview to render dependency chains as an indented tree rather than a flat list, making the critical path immediately visible.

title: Add --outcome flag to domus task create and update
summary: Add an --outcome flag to both 'domus task create' and 'domus task update'. Flags should mirror between create and update — adding a flag to one means adding it to the other.

title: Enforce task closure as part of worker lifecycle
summary: Nothing currently ties a commit to a task status update, so tasks stay open after work lands. Task closure must be a required structural step in the dispatch/worker flow, not a discipline item.

title: Prompt to update depends-on when capturing a new prerequisite task
summary: When a new task is captured that is a prerequisite for existing tasks, nothing prompts updating downstream depends-on fields. This silently corrupts the dependency graph.

title: Add skill syncing to domus init
summary: Ship a reference skills/ directory in the domus repo (starting with land-worktree). During domus init, discover which skills domus provides, compare with what is already installed in the project's .claude/skills/, and copy in the delta. Idempotent: re-running init only adds missing or updated skills.

title: Implement project memory in .domus/memory/
summary: Move project memory into .domus/memory/ so it travels with the repo. Covers: domus init creates .domus/memory/MEMORY.md and patches agents.md with @-include; update capture skill to route project vs local memory silently and announce which store was written to; migrate existing project/reference memory from local Claude memory into .domus/memory/ for the domus project itself. See ADR 007.

title: Align execution pipeline with ADR 004/005 design
summary: Epic tracker for bringing the domus codebase into alignment with ADR 004 (store and worker logging) and ADR 005 (execution engine and progress mobility). No implementation work of its own — done when all subtasks are complete.
outcome_note: All 3 subtasks complete and merged into feat/rework-domus-init.

title: Execution engine and progress mobility implementation
summary: Implement the dispatch interface, task lifecycle commands, and status values per ADR 005. Covers: refactor dispatch to take task-id, add domus task start, add domus task log, add ready-for-senior-review and ready-for-manual-review status values.
outcome_note: All 3 constituent tasks done. Subtask 2 (worker-autonomy) provided the DOMUS_ROOT resolution that completed the dependency chain.

title: Worker autonomy and execution model alignment
summary: Fix the worker execution model to match ADR 004. Covers: fix worker log path to .domus/execution-logs/<task-id>.md, fix branch detection to use main, add DOMUS_ROOT persistent config to domus init, add execution-logs/ to DOMUS_DIRS.
outcome_note: Work was already committed on feat/rework-domus-init from a prior session. Worker verified all 6 acceptance criteria met.

title: Role files and session routing implementation
summary: Write the three stub role files (Worker, Foreman, Taskmaster) and fix session routing. Covers: full Worker/Foreman/Taskmaster role file content, verify Butler routing from role-activation-rules.md, fix herald.md and doctor.md execution log path references.
outcome_note: Full role files written for worker, foreman, taskmaster. Herald path fixed. Merged into feat/rework-domus-init.
